rationale: Most bead surfaces are empirically real: workspace accessors and TreeRootForSpecDir have the described canonical shape; validate docsync/divergence/ownership matchers, LoadOwnershipAtRef/domainManifestRelPath, and contextpack hardcodes exist; internal/layout is absent; panel Scan/PanelDirScanRoot, doctor migration/docs, migrate.go rubric, glossary/policies, domain self-globs, and ADR-0039-next are all grounded. However Bead 4 names a nonexistent internal/complete MergeBranch and omits the real spec-to-main merge surfaces. Bead 3 also claims git-mv/reset routing through internal/executor without including the executor/gitutil API work needed to make that possible.
concrete_changes_required: Bead 4 must replace the nonexistent internal/complete MergeBranch reference with the actual merge surfaces: complete.Run before exec.CompleteBead for bead-to-spec, and the impl-approve/executor FinalizeEpic path for spec-to-main, or explicitly add an executor-level guard covering both.
Bead 4 key_file_paths and tests must include the real files needed for spec-to-main merge guarding, not only internal/complete.

Bead 3 must either add internal/executor and/or internal/gitutil API changes to its scope for git mv, hard reset, branch/ref discovery, and rollback, or revise the design to a repo-real seam that already exists.

findings.issue: Plan step 2 says to add the layout-fingerprint hard-fail to internal/complete MergeBranch, but no such function exists. The raw helpers are internal/gitutil.MergeBranch/MergeInto, while complete.Run only calls exec.CompleteBead; spec-to-main finalization flows through internal/approve to executor.FinalizeEpic.

The plan says the mover's git-mv driver is routed through internal/executor, but the current Executor interface has no git mv, hard-reset, clean, or ref-discovery primitives, and Bead 3 does not list internal/executor or internal/gitutil as changed surfaces.
